name: Classic Mashed Potatoes
tagline: Yukon golds riced with butter and warm cream
highlight: Riced for cloud-like texture — no soy, no gum, just butter and cream

hero.alt: Bowl of fluffy mashed potatoes with butter pat
hero.caption: mashed potatoes — riced and silky

healthFacts: Yukon golds have less starch than russets — silkier mash.
Warm cream + soft butter = velvet; cold dairy seizes the starch.

steps.title: Boil whole
steps.detail: Quarter potatoes if very large; otherwise leave whole. Cover with cold salted water; bring to a boil. Simmer 18–22 min until fork-tender.
steps.tip: Cold-water start cooks evenly. Hot start = mushy outside, raw center.

steps.title: Drain and dry
steps.detail: Drain. Return to the warm pot to evaporate excess water for 30 sec.
steps.tip: Wet potatoes = watery mash. Dry first.

steps.title: Rice through
steps.detail: Rice or push potatoes through a food mill back into the pot. Even masher works — but ricer wins.
steps.tip: No ricer? Use the largest holes of a box grater for a similar effect.

steps.title: Fold in butter, then cream
steps.detail: Fold butter in piece by piece. Gradually fold in warm cream and milk. Salt and white pepper to taste.
steps.tip: Folding (not beating) keeps starch from over-developing. Beating = glue mash.
